title: The Outline of History
description: A sweeping popular history of humanity written in an internationalist and anti-dogmatic spirit.
descriptionParagraphs: A sweeping popular history of humanity written in an internationalist and anti-dogmatic spirit.

The Outline of History is organized less as a story than as an argument. As a history, it tries to persuade readers through selection, emphasis, and direct claims about history, internationalism, and anti nationalism.

Its significance lies in the way it compresses large claims into memorable formulas and positions. Even readers who reject the work usually have to reckon with how sharply it frames questions about history, internationalism, and anti nationalism.

cover.alt: The Outline of History cover thumbnail from Wikipedia

overview.englishDescription: A sweeping popular history of humanity written in an internationalist and anti-dogmatic spirit.
banningParagraphs: The Outline of History entered censorship debates as a history associated with history, internationalism, and anti nationalism. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around anti fascism and ideological control.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1930s in Germany, where Nazi authorities banned circulation. Wells's internationalist history clashed with racial and nationalist doctrine. Broad synthetic histories often become targets because they frame the past differently from official ideology.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
